# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

Prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.140.0/http/mod.ts";

interface Config {
  modulePaths?: string[];
}

async function main() {
  const config = getConfig();
  const modules = await loadModules(config.modulePaths);
  const handler = async (req: Request) => {
    try {
      const { pathname } = new URL(req.url);
      if (pathname === "/") {
        return new Response("Welcome to the plugin system!");
      }
      const modulePath = req.url.split("/").pop();
      if (!modulePath) {
        return new Response("Module not found", { status: 404 });
      }
      const module = await import(modulePath);
      return new Response(module.default());
    } catch (error) {
      console.error(error);
      return new Response("Error loading module", { status: 500 });
    }
  };

  serve(handler, { port: 8080 });
}

function getConfig(): Config {
  const envModulePaths = Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATHS");
  if (envModulePaths) {
    return JSON.parse(envModulePaths);
  }

  const queryModulePaths = new URLSearchParams(Deno.query).get("module_paths");
  if (queryModulePaths) {
    return JSON.parse(queryModulePaths);
  }

  return {};
}

async function loadModules(paths: string[]): Promise<Record<string, () => unknown>> {
  const modules: Record<string, () => unknown> = {};

  for (const path of paths) {
    try {
      const module = await import(path);
      modules[path] = module.default;
    } catch (error) {
      console.error(`Error loading module ${path}:`, error);
    }
  }

  return modules;
}

main();
